description_long: This is a rustic wooden directional signpost set in a circular patch of grass, featuring a weathered vertical post with several horizontal planks attached at different heights and angles. Each plank serves as a signboard, with white painted text and arrows indicating directions to various locations such as 'Bird Hide', 'Diversity Trail', 'Shelter', 'South Entrance', and 'Eco-Toilet'. The wood appears aged and rough, with visible grain and natural imperfections, giving it a natural, outdoor aesthetic. The signpost is firmly embedded in the ground, suggesting it is intended to be a permanent fixture. The text and arrows are hand-painted, adding to the informal, park-like feel. The overall appearance suggests it is designed for use in a public park, nature reserve, or similar green space.
description: A weathered wooden signpost with multiple hand-painted directional planks and arrows, set in a grassy patch. The sign features white text pointing to various park locations.
description_view_0: Front view showing three signboards with visible white text and arrows, clearly displaying directions to 'Bird Hide', 'Diversity Trail', and 'Eco-Toilet'.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden planks and the rough texture of the post, with partial visibility of the text and arrows.
description_view_2: Rear view where the text is not visible, showing mostly the back of the planks and the post embedded in grass.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the 'Bird Hide' and 'Diversity Trail' signs, with the post and grassy base prominent.
attribute_text_content: BIRD HIDE DIVERSITY TRAIL SHELTER SOUTH ENTRANCE ECO-TOILET
